title: Fix 'Failed to load' error across Poetry, Dua, and Songs sections

requirements.summary: Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks to correctly call backend methods, transform Candid responses, and handle errors gracefully
acceptanceCriteria: useGetAllPoetry calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
useGetAllDua calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
useGetAllSongs calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
A network or canister error sets isError=true and does not throw an unhandled exception
Each hook uses a stable, unique query key

file_operations.description: Fix useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs hooks to call the exact backend method names (getAllPoetry, getAllDua, getAllSongs) as defined in backend-interface. Ensure each hook uses a stable, unique query key. Add explicit try/catch error handling in queryFn to return user-friendly error states instead of throwing unhandled exceptions. Verify that the Candid response field names (id, title, content, likes, category, image/audio) match the TypeScript type definitions and map them correctly if needed. Ensure the likes field is properly transformed from the backend Likes type (count: bigint, likedBy: Array<string>) to match frontend expectations.

requirements.summary: Verify and fix PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components to correctly handle loading, error, and empty states based on actual data shapes from fixed hooks
acceptanceCriteria: PoetryPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
DuaPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
SongsPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
No valid backend response is incorrectly treated as an error due to field-name mismatches

file_operations.description: Review the PoetryPage component to ensure it correctly handles the data shape returned by useGetAllPoetry after REQ-42 fixes. Verify that loading state shows skeletons, error state displays error message with retry button, and success state renders PoetryPost cards. Remove any field-name assumptions or type mismatches that cause valid responses to be treated as errors. Ensure the component correctly accesses poetry.likes.count for display.

Review the DuaPage component to ensure it correctly handles the data shape returned by useGetAllDua after REQ-42 fixes. Verify that loading state shows skeletons, error state displays error message with retry button, and success state renders DuaPost cards. Remove any field-name assumptions or type mismatches that cause valid responses to be treated as errors. Ensure the component correctly accesses dua.likes.count for display.

Review the SongsPage component to ensure it correctly handles the data shape returned by useGetAllSongs after REQ-42 fixes. Verify that loading state shows skeletons, error state displays error message with retry button, and success state renders SongPost cards. Remove any field-name assumptions or type mismatches that cause valid responses to be treated as errors. Note that Song type does not have a likes field according to backend-interface, so ensure the component does not expect one.

requirements.summary: Fix the MixPage component to correctly aggregate data from all three hooks after REQ-42 fixes and render a unified trending feed with appropriate loading and error states
acceptanceCriteria: MixPage shows a loading state while any of the three data sources are loading
MixPage renders a unified feed of posts sorted by like count when data is available
MixPage shows an error state only when all three data sources fail

file_operations.description: Fix the MixPage component to correctly aggregate Poetry, Dua, and Song data from useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs hooks after REQ-42 fixes. Ensure loading state is shown while any query is loading. Combine available data from all three sources and sort by like count (note: songs do not have likes, so handle them separately or sort them to the end). Render PoetryPost, DuaPost, and SongPost components appropriately. Show error state only when all three queries fail. Ensure the component correctly accesses likes.count for Poetry and Dua items when sorting.
